title: LR-Auth: Towards Practical Implementation of Implicit User Authentication on Earbuds
venue: Proc. ACM Interact. Mob. Wearable Ubiquitous Technol.

method.input_representation: linear correlation ratios of audio streams across frequencies
method.training_strategy: Template generation via linear regression on synthesized audio stimulus

evaluation.context: Achieved in Scenario 1 (speaker off, noisy environment) using a single 100ms frame

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-subject-out cross-validation (1 legitimate user vs 29 imposters)

reported_limitations: Ear inflammation alters ear canal shape
Jaw movements during speaking and eating disrupt linear correlation
Insufficient energy in quiet environments without music

usability.usability_issues: Requires audio signal energy (music or noise) to function

key_contribution: Lightweight implicit authentication using linear correlation of ear canal acoustic modulation
Synthesis of a 1-second stimulus for rapid enrollment
Scenario-adaptive authentication templates for noise and music
main_strength: Extremely low computational overhead (latency and energy)
High accuracy across multiple real-world scenarios
main_weakness: Dependency on minimum ambient/internal sound levels
insights.notes_for_review: The system is highly efficient but relies on the physics of acoustic impedance; it is not a traditional ML-based biometric system.
